title: Incept AP Coverage Dashboard
subtitle: Where the closed learning loop works today, by AP subject.

data_sources.retest_links: AP Classroom: apclassroom.collegeboard.org · Barron's: barronseduc.com/resources/active · Princeton/UWorld/URP/Fiveable/Albert: TBD (see timeback-loops/src/components/RetestCard/copy.js for the canonical RETEST_COPY[].link map)

loop_stages.pretest: Has the student mastered the prerequisites? A gating check before practice begins. Today no subject gates on prereq mastery — recommendations flow from current performance, not prereq state. Universal product gap.
loop_stages.diagnose: Wrong answers → content hash → AP standard tags via the loops_question_tag map. Requires CED file + tagger coverage.
loop_stages.diagnostic: Can we tell the student 'you know X%, need Y XP to completion' from a knowledge map? Different from Diagnose (which is reactive — runs after a wrong answer). Diagnostic is a forward-looking estimator. Today no subject has this — the system can recommend the next test but can't quote XP-to-completion.
loop_stages.score: Once a test is taken, the FRQ grader scores the rubric and the bucket re-evaluates. Closed for every subject that has a working grader (full or calibrated fallback). Open only for subjects with no grader path at all.
loop_stages.decide: Compute the next-test recommendation from current signal (nextTest.js). Subject-agnostic; works wherever the bucket logic has data to read.
loop_stages.act: Build Knowledge → incept-hole-filler generates a tailored remediation course, pushes it to TimeBack, auto-enrolls the student. Requires the subject to be registered in the hole-filler.
loop_stages.retest: Assign a new test to the student. MVP is link+nudge for every source: for AP Classroom, 'Ask your guide to assign Practice Exam 2'; for Barron's, 'Take Barron's Test 2 [deep link]'; same pattern for Princeton, UWorld, URP, Fiveable, Albert when those wire in. No platform-access APIs are needed — we initially thought that was the blocker, but it isn't. Today the next-test recommendation displays on the dashboard but nothing nudges the student. The plan at ap-test-scrapper/docs/plans/exhaustion-detection-plan.md ships the recommender + render layer in ~2 weeks end-to-end, flipping this stage from 'open' to 'partial' for the 12 CB-live subjects. Full 'closed' (automated email/Slack nudge on readiness) is a smaller follow-up. The Common Core Crawl is the longer-term path to owning the assignment surface end-to-end (all 7 sources in AlphaTest) but it's NOT blocking the MVP.

status_legend.closed: Fully automated end-to-end; no human in the loop required.
status_legend.partial: Wired up but with a known caveat (e.g., grader uses generic fallback prompt, low student validation count).
status_legend.open: Not wired up or known broken; requires manual intervention or has no path.

integration_legend.live: Wired into production; ingestion is running.
integration_legend.partial: Scraper or adapter feature-complete, but not yet wired into the production publisher pipeline.
integration_legend.open: Not started — placeholder for the Common Core Crawl roadmap.

contradictions.title: Re-test fully closed only when an automated nudge fires — link surface ships but no email/Slack ping yet
contradictions.details: RetestCard ships in timeback-loops (PR #29) and renders source-appropriate copy + CTA link on every per-student page. AP Classroom recommendations say 'ask their guide to assign Practice Exam 2'; Barron's recommendations carry a deep link; same shape for Princeton/UWorld/etc when those wire in. Both producers are now wired end-to-end (CB via cbscraper, Barron's via Akshit's bidirectional scraper that also pushes student score reports back to TimeBack as ALIs). 16 of 17 subjects have at least one live source; AP Music Theory has neither and remains universally open. The remaining gap to 'closed' is the automated nudge — nothing yet emails the guide or pings Slack when readiness is detected; the card just waits for someone to look at it.
contradictions.remediation: Smaller follow-up: add an automated nudge (email or Slack ping) when (a) a student's last-test scoreDate is older than the cadence threshold AND (b) RetestCard would render a non-empty recommendation. Cadence threshold lives in nextTestDate(); reuse it. Surface = whichever channel guides actually read. Once that ships, flip loop.retest from 'partial' to 'closed' for the 16 subjects.

contradictions.title: AP Calculus BC has enrolled students but no FRQ grader
contradictions.details: 2 students sit the May 11 exam. The slug is not registered in incept-hole-filler/src/hole_filler/subjects.py, and engine/regrade/subject_registry.py raises KeyError on it. MCQ ingestion + tagger work via the CED file, but Build Knowledge cannot generate a tailored course and FRQ sections cannot be auto-scored.
contradictions.remediation: Either (a) add Calc BC to both registries before May 11, or (b) flag the 2 affected students for manual remediation.

contradictions.title: AP Music Theory is universally open — no CB practice exams, no Barron's scrape, no CED tagger
contradictions.details: Only subject without ANY wired integration source. CB has no practice exams in this school's classroom; Barron's didn't scrape it (no CED-aligned content available); ap-test-scrapper has no CED file in data/ced_ap_*.json so the tagger has no standards to map to. RetestCard renders the no-recommendation fallback ('all wired sources have been exhausted'). 1 student affected.
contradictions.remediation: If the cohort grows, source CED-aligned Music Theory content from another provider (Princeton Review or Ultimate Review Packet are most likely candidates per the Common Core Crawl roadmap). Until then, this student's remediation is manual.
